task_completion.description: Verify that a lookup table file has been created in Splunk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a new CSV file listed in the Splunk 'Settings' -> 'Lookups' -> 'Lookup table files' page, or a search query successfully executing the 'outputlookup' command.

task_completion.description: Verify the lookup table contains statistics for at least the top 5 most active users.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Splunk search or table view showing the contents of the lookup file (e.g., using '| inputlookup <filename>'), displaying at least 5 rows of user statistics.

task_completion.description: Verify that a lookup definition has been configured for the created lookup table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a new entry in the Splunk 'Settings' -> 'Lookups' -> 'Lookup definitions' page, linked to the created lookup file.

task_completion.description: Verify that a saved report has been created for the user authentication activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a newly saved report listed in the 'Reports' tab of the Splunk Search & Reporting app.

task_completion.description: Verify that the saved report uses the lookup to produce a structured view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search string of the saved report (either in the report execution view or edit view) and ensure it contains the 'lookup' or 'inputlookup' command.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk Web interface (http://localhost:8000) being navigated, rather than terminal windows using curl or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent queried the actual security_logs index to generate the lookup data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a search query in the Splunk search bar containing 'index=security_logs' (e.g., 'index=security_logs | top 5 user | outputlookup...').

integrity.description: Verify the lookup wasn't manually populated with fake data via a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The lookup creation should stem from a Splunk search ('outputlookup') or Splunk GUI upload, not a manual terminal file creation (e.g., using 'echo' or 'nano' to create a CSV).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target index: 'security_logs'. Minimum lookup rows required: 5. Splunk credentials: 'admin' / 'SplunkAdmin1!'. The scoring requires 5 specific elements: lookup file created, lookup file has at least 5 rows, lookup definition configured, report created, and the report uses the lookup.
